target.name: Harbinger
target.founder: John Harris, Phillip Weicker, Will Eberts

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

data.narrative:
Harbinger, a Series C transportation company founded by John Harris, Phillip Weicker, and Will Eberts, is valued at $500M post-money and has raised $358M to date. The relevant peer universe consists of 7 private companies in transportation technology, including Einride, Rivian, The Boring Company, REGENT Craft, and ZeroAvia. No public comparables exist in a sufficiently narrow peer set.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as peer-median triangulation cannot be applied. The target transportation sector contains only 1 same-stage comparable and 0 adjacent-stage peers meeting sufficiently narrow filters; a broader all-sector approach would introduce material distortion. Stage-progression applies a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C advancement to the $500M base, yielding an implied valuation of $850M.

The analysis supports a range of $722M to $977M, centered at $850M. This narrow range reflects the analytical constraint: only one applicable method produced the triangulation, limiting confidence in precision. Key uncertainties include (1) the applicability of the 1.7x step-up multiplier to Harbinger's specific market position and growth trajectory relative to unstated peer progression patterns, and (2) absence of comparable transaction data or public market signals in transportation technology. Further diligence on peer financing velocity and Harbinger's unit economics relative to the identified private cohort would strengthen conviction in this range.
